source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Crane Incident at Ship Shoal Block 72 Platform OF

event.top_event: Loss of Load - Crane Wire Rope Failure
actions_taken: Air compressor recovered from offshore waters on December 18, 2021
Main line winch and main line wire rope replaced and placed back in service on December 17, 2021
Aux winch and aux line replaced on January 12, 2022
Crane placed back into full service after repairs
event.summary: On December 13, 2021 at 11:15 hours, the auxiliary wire rope of the SS 72 OF crane failed during offloading operations at Sanare Energy Partners' Ship Shoal Block 72 Platform. A 4,000 lbs rental air compressor being offloaded from motor vessel Leader fell approximately 20-30 feet into offshore waters when the aux cable parted above the hook. The compressor was recovered five days later. Root cause was determined to be failure to lubricate the wire rope, allowing corrosion and disintegration. Contributing factors included inadequate maintenance, failure to follow manufacturer recommendations, and failure to take the crane out of service despite identified deficiencies.
recommendations: Ensure cranes with identified deficiencies are taken out of service or operation is restricted in accordance with API RP 2D paragraph 3.1.5c
Implement proper lubrication program for wire rope to prevent corrosion
Maintain mechanical integrity program in accordance with manufacturer recommendations
Ensure crane service providers follow established procedures and API standards
Post proper cautionary notices and communicate limits of continued service when deficiencies are identified

threats.name: Failure to Replace Degraded Wire Rope

consequences.name: Loss of Load - Air Compressor Falls into Water
Environmental Impact - Equipment in Offshore Waters

controls.name: Wire Rope Lubrication Program
controls.barrier_role: Prevent wire rope corrosion and degradation
controls.lod_basis: Planned maintenance program to extend wire rope service life

supporting_text: Sanare's NTO Report determined that the Root Cause of the crane incident was their failure to lubricate the wire rope allowing for corrosion and disintegration
the aux line was not well greased, heavily corroded at the cable end
wire rope manufacturer made several recommendations regarding maintenance strategies to improve or increase service life of the wire rope

controls.name: Annual Crane Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identify wire rope and equipment deficiencies
controls.lod_basis: API RP 2D requirement for annual inspection of infrequent usage cranes

supporting_text: An Annual Crane Inspection of the OF Crane was completed on July 17, 2021 by a CM working for Phoenix Offshore Solutions, LLC

the CM did not place the aux line out of service despite indicating in the Annual Inspection Report that the aux line was not well greased, heavily corroded at the cable end, and that both the aux wire rope and aux hoist had well surpassed the service limit
the CM indicated that new aux wire rope and a new aux hoist needed to be ordered on a Service Parts Requisition Form

controls.name: Level 5 Teardown Inspection and Magnetic Particle Test (MPI)
controls.barrier_role: Detect internal winch degradation and defects
controls.lod_basis: Winch manufacturer bulletin requirement for inspection every 5 years

supporting_text: The aux winch in service at the time of the incident was last certified on June 22, 2012
the bulletin from the winch manufacturer states that the aux winch should have undergone a Level 5 teardown inspection and Magnetic Particle Test (MPI) in June of 2017
According to the Sanare provided SS 72 OF crane records, the Level 5 Inspection and MPI were not completed per the Bulletin specifications

controls.name: Crane Out of Service (OOS) Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent operation of crane with identified deficiencies
controls.lod_basis: API RP 2D paragraph 3.1.5c requirement to take crane OOS when deficiencies impair safe operation

supporting_text: Despite the CM's noted findings during the Annual OF Crane Inspection, the CM did not take the OF crane out of service (OOS), post proper cautionary notices, or communicate limits of continued service
the CM defended inaction stating that the aux line passed inspection and that there were no apparent safety concerns warranting the crane be removed from service

BSEE found that Sanare and the crane service provider failed to take the SS 72 OF crane OOS, post proper cautionary notices, or communicate limits of continued service after deficiencies were identified

controls.name: Wire Rope Replacement Program
controls.barrier_role: Replace degraded wire rope before failure
controls.lod_basis: Maintenance program to replace wire rope when service limits exceeded

supporting_text: the CM included new aux wire rope in a Service Parts Requisition list completed on July 17, 2021
Phoenix sent Sanare a quote for approximately $30,000 in new OF crane parts and materials on July 20, 2021
Sanare did not approve the quote until after the December 13, 2021 aux wire rope failure
the aux wire rope had been in service for 9 years since July 3, 2012

controls.name: Pre-Use Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Verify crane condition before each use
controls.lod_basis: API RP 2D requirement for infrequent usage cranes with special attention to wire rope

supporting_text: On December 13, 2021, a certified CO completed a Pre-Use Inspection
API RP 2D states that cranes in the Infrequent Usage Category be subject to a Pre-Use and Annual Inspection
API RP 2D also specifies that special attention should be given to wire rope on Infrequent Usage Cranes during Pre-Use Inspections

controls.name: Mechanical Integrity Program
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is maintained per manufacturer specifications
controls.lod_basis: Operator responsibility to maintain equipment per manufacturer recommendations

supporting_text: Sanare failed to maintain the OF crane per manufacturer's recommendations and failed to identify and address deficiencies
Sanare also identified that they failed to manage and maintain their mechanical integrity program
BSEE found that the aux hoist was not maintained in accordance with the aux winch manufacturer's recommendations

controls.name: Crane Service Provider Procedure Compliance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure service provider follows established procedures and API standards
controls.lod_basis: Contractor responsibility to follow API RP 2D and operator procedures

supporting_text: the crane service provider failed to follow their own procedures as well as those found in API RP 2D
the Phoenix CM reported that this task was not completed. There was no documentation in the crane records supporting why the Phoenix CM chose to not cut and remake the aux line
the Phoenix CM reported to BSEE on June 1, 2022 that he chose to not cut and remove 15' of aux wire rope during the Annual Inspection because the cable was written up to be changed

controls.name: Load Monitoring and Communication
controls.barrier_role: Detect load failure and alert personnel
controls.lod_basis: Personnel awareness of load status during lifting operations

supporting_text: Witness statements of the riggers involved and the CO all reported hearing or seeing the line part and/or the compressor hitting the water
Following the incident the crane was shutdown and was not moved

controls.name: Equipment Recovery Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Recover lost equipment from offshore waters

supporting_text: The air compressor was recovered from offshore waters on December 18, 2021
BSEE was informed that a dive team was scheduled to recover the rental air compressor on December 18, 2021

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
